coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] Gerrit merge evidence and approval trail will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: Gerrit merge evidence and approval trail

coaching.coaching: Pin down exact current content of pyproject.toml pin comment with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: exact current content of pyproject.toml pin comment

coaching.coaching: Plan how completeness and verifiability of acceptance criteria will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: completeness and verifiability of acceptance criteria

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Prefix the Gerrit merge AC with `[operator-attested]` so ADR 0043's attestation exception formally applies and the criterion's evidence (Gerrit change-id + vote record) is accepted as its proving artifact.
evidence: Plan text: 'The change is merged to `main` via Gerrit with `LLM-Review +1` and `Verified +1`'
ADR 0043: a criterion tagged `[operator-attested]` is met by a recorded attestation (change id / vote / timestamp) — the tag is required for the exception to apply
ABSENCE: the checkbox text does not begin with `[operator-attested]`
findings.finding: The final acceptance criterion ('The change is merged to `main` via Gerrit with `LLM-Review +1` and `Verified +1`') requires a governance/process outcome whose evidence lives outside the repo (a Gerrit vote record), yet it lacks the `[operator-attested]` tag that ADR 0043 requires for such criteria. Without the tag, the E6 operator-attested exception doesn't formally apply and the criterion has no in-session proving command, making it technically unverifiable in-session under F1.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – last bullet: 'The change is merged to `main` via Gerrit with `LLM-Review +1` and `Verified +1`'
scenarios: A strict automated reviewer flags this AC as having no in-session proving command
An agent completing the plan cannot mark this criterion done without leaving the session to check Gerrit
findings.suggested_fix: Change the last AC to: `- [ ] [operator-attested] The change is merged to \`main\` via Gerrit with \`LLM-Review +1\` and \`Verified +1\` (record the Gerrit change-id here as attestation).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct Plan step 1 and AC #3: the comment above `zizmor==1.27.0` does NOT contain the string `1.27.0`; remove the claim that it does, and rewrite AC #3 to verify the actual pin value change (e.g. 'the `zizmor==` pin in `pyproject.toml` reads `1.28.0`, not `1.27.0`') so the AC is non-vacuous.
evidence: Plan step 1: 'refresh the pin comment that names 1.27.0'
AC #3: 'The `[dev]` pin comment above zizmor no longer references the string `1.27.0`'

pyproject.toml lines 187-191 comment text: 'zizmor tightens/adds audits across releases, so an unpinned floor lets findings drift (green here, red in CI). Bump deliberately.' — contains no occurrence of '1.27.0'
The version string '1.27.0' appears ONLY in the pin value on line 192: `"zizmor==1.27.0"`, not in any comment
findings.finding: Plan step 1 asserts 'refresh the pin comment that names 1.27.0', and AC #3 asserts 'The [dev] pin comment above zizmor no longer references the string 1.27.0' — but probing pyproject.toml lines 187-191 (the comment block immediately above `zizmor==1.27.0`) shows no occurrence of the string '1.27.0' in the comment text. The comment reads only 'zizmor tightens/adds audits across releases, so an unpinned floor lets findings drift (green here, red in CI). Bump deliberately.' AC #3 is therefore already vacuously satisfied (no change needed to pass it), making it a misleading/false acceptance criterion, and step 1 describes comment-update work that does not actually exist.
findings.location: Plan section 'Plan' step 1; AC line 3 ('The `[dev]` pin comment above zizmor no longer references the string `1.27.0`'); pyproject.toml lines 187-192
scenarios: An implementer searches for '1.27.0' in the comment block to update it, finds nothing, and is confused about whether there is missing work.
AC #3 passes on the current (unmodified) file, making it a non-verifying acceptance criterion that provides no confidence the pin was actually bumped.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove 'refresh the pin comment that names 1.27.0' from Plan step 1 (no such comment exists). Replace AC #3 with a verifiable check on the pin value itself, e.g.: '`pyproject.toml` contains `zizmor==1.28.0` and does NOT contain `zizmor==1.27.0`' — this is distinct from AC #1 (which checks the line exists) by also asserting the old value is gone, or simply drop AC #3 as redundant with AC #1.

evidence: AC item 'The change is merged to `main` via Gerrit with `LLM-Review +1` and `Verified +1`' cites operational evidence (merge_gate) that lives outside the codebase but is not tagged [operator-attested]; prefix the checkbox text with [operator-attested] so the completion verifier accepts a recorded attestation instead of failing to find code proof (ADR-0043).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
